symptom_probes.pain: Have you been having any pain or discomfort anywhere else in your body?
symptom_probes.headache: Have you been getting headaches or head pressure?
symptom_probes.chest: Have you had any chest discomfort, tightness, or pain?
symptom_probes.fatigue: How has your energy been? Have you been feeling more tired than usual?
symptom_probes.tired: How has your energy been? Have you been feeling more tired than usual?
symptom_probes.dyspnea: Have you had any trouble breathing or felt short of breath?
symptom_probes.breath: Have you had any trouble breathing or felt short of breath?
symptom_probes.cough: Have you had a cough — dry or productive?
symptom_probes.fever: Have you had any fever, chills, or night sweats?
symptom_probes.nausea: Have you felt nauseous or had any vomiting?
symptom_probes.vomiting: Have you felt nauseous or had any vomiting?
symptom_probes.diarrhea: Have you had any changes in your bowel movements — loose stools or diarrhea?
symptom_probes.constipat: Have you had any changes in your bowel movements — constipation?
symptom_probes.dizziness: Have you felt dizzy or lightheaded at all?
symptom_probes.vertigo: Have you experienced any dizziness or a spinning sensation?
symptom_probes.swelling: Have you noticed any swelling, particularly in your legs or ankles?
symptom_probes.edema: Have you noticed any swelling or fluid retention?
symptom_probes.rash: Have you developed any skin rashes or unusual spots?
symptom_probes.itch: Have you had any itching that seems unusual?
symptom_probes.urinary: Have you noticed any changes in your urination — frequency, color, or discomfort?
symptom_probes.numbness: Have you experienced any numbness, tingling, or pins-and-needles sensations?
symptom_probes.tingling: Have you experienced any numbness, tingling, or pins-and-needles sensations?
symptom_probes.vision: Have you noticed any changes in your vision — blurriness, spots, or double vision?
symptom_probes.sleep: How has your sleep been? Any trouble falling or staying asleep?
symptom_probes.insomnia: How has your sleep been? Any trouble falling or staying asleep?
symptom_probes.appetite: Has your appetite changed recently — eating more or less than usual?
symptom_probes.weight: Have you noticed any unexpected weight changes — gain or loss?
symptom_probes.anxiety: Have you been feeling anxious, worried, or on edge?
symptom_probes.depress: How has your mood been? Have you been feeling down or lost interest in things?
symptom_probes.joint: Have you had any joint pain, stiffness, or swelling?
symptom_probes.muscle: Have you had any muscle aches, weakness, or soreness?
symptom_probes.infection: Have you been getting sick more often than usual — more colds, infections, or fevers?
symptom_probes.bleed: Have you noticed any unusual bleeding or bruising?
symptom_probes.palpit: Have you noticed your heart racing, pounding, or skipping beats?
symptom_probes.syncope: Have you had any episodes of fainting or feeling like you might pass out?
symptom_probes.sweat: Have you been experiencing unusual sweating, especially at night?
symptom_probes.thirst: Have you been more thirsty than usual?
symptom_probes.urinat: Have you been urinating more frequently than usual?
symptom_probes.memory: Have you had any trouble with your memory or concentration?
symptom_probes.confusion: Have you felt confused or had difficulty concentrating?
symptom_probes.weakness: Have you noticed any weakness in your arms or legs?

opening: Doctor, I’ve been having {symptom} for about {duration}. I thought I should get it checked out.
Hi doctor, I’m here because of {symptom}. It started about {duration} ago and I’m a bit concerned.
Good morning doctor. I’ve been experiencing {symptom} for the past {duration}. I wanted to come in sooner but it didn’t seem that bad at first.
Hello doctor, thanks for seeing me. My main issue is {symptom}, which has been bothering me for about {duration} now.
Doctor, I noticed {symptom} about {duration} ago. It’s been getting progressively worse so I made an appointment.
history_response: Yes, I was diagnosed with {condition} about {time_ago}. I’m currently taking {medication}.
I have a history of {condition}. My doctor prescribed {medication} for it.
Right, I was told I have {condition} back in {time_ago}. I take {medication} for it and it’s been well controlled.
Yes, {condition}. I was diagnosed {time_ago}. I’m on {medication} and I try to manage it with lifestyle changes too.
symptom_response: Yes, the {symptom_detail} is {severity}. It gets worse when {trigger}.
The {symptom_detail} is pretty consistent. I’d say it’s about {severity_level} out of 10.
It varies throughout the day. In the morning it’s manageable, but by evening the {symptom_detail} is definitely worse.
The {symptom_detail} comes and goes. I notice it most after {trigger}. I’d rate it around {severity_level} out of 10.
agreement: Yes, I understand. I’ll follow your advice.
That makes sense. What should I do next?
OK, thank you doctor. I appreciate your help.
Got it. I’ll make sure to do that. When should I come back?
That sounds reasonable. Are there any side effects I should watch for?
Understood. Thank you for explaining everything clearly.
no_history: No, I don’t have any significant medical history.
I’ve been generally healthy before this.
Not that I know of. I haven’t been hospitalized before.
no_allergies: No allergies that I know of.
No known drug allergies. I’ve taken antibiotics before without issues.

opening: Doctor, I have this... um... {symptom}, I think. It’s been going on for... a while? Maybe {duration}?
I’m not feeling well. Something about {symptom}. My wife told me to come. It’s been... some time.
My daughter made me come in. I think it’s {symptom}? How long has it been... I’m not sure. A few weeks maybe?
I keep forgetting to call the doctor. It’s the {symptom} thing... Started around... hmm, {duration} I want to say.
Oh yes, I’m here because of... let me think... {symptom}. My neighbor noticed it first. It’s been going on for some time now.
history_response: I think I had something before... let me think... Oh yes, the doctor said something about {condition}. I take pills but I forget the name... maybe {medication}?
There might be something in my records. I don’t remember exactly, but I think {condition}.
My wife handles all my medications. She’d know about the {condition}. I just take what’s in my pill organizer.
I have a list somewhere... the doctor said {condition}, I believe. I take {medication} for it, I think.
symptom_response: Hmm, I’m not sure about the details. My wife says I’ve been having {symptom_detail}. I can’t really remember when it started exactly.
It’s hard to describe... The {symptom_detail} is there sometimes. I don’t know if it’s {severity} or not.
I notice it most in the morning, I think? Or is it at night? The {symptom_detail} comes and goes.
My son says I should pay more attention to these things. The {symptom_detail} has been bothering me for... I’m not sure how long.
agreement: What was that again? I’m sorry, I didn’t catch that.
OK... I think I understand. Can you write that down for me?
I’ll try to remember that. What was the medicine called again?
Right, right. I’ll do my best to remember. Can you repeat the last part?
Mhm, I think I got it. So what do I need to do exactly?
I see. Let me just make sure... what was the main thing I need to do?
Oh yes, that sounds familiar. I think my wife mentioned something about that.
Got it. I’ll write it down when I get home so I don’t forget.
Can you say that one more time? I want to tell my daughter the details.
I’ll put a reminder in my phone. What time should I take the medication?
no_history: I don’t think so... but I’m not sure.
My doctor might have said something once, but I can’t recall.
no_allergies: I don’t think so... my daughter would know.
Not that I remember, but I’m not sure.
Nobody’s ever told me I’m allergic to anything.

opening: Doctor, I have this {symptom} thing, and I looked it up online and it said it could be something serious? Is it cancer?
I have {symptom} and my neighbor said it means {wrong_diagnosis}. Is that true? Should I be worried?
I was reading on WebMD that {symptom} can be really dangerous. Should I be scared? What if it’s something terrible?
My friend had the same {symptom} and she ended up in the hospital. Am I going to be okay? What tests do I need?
I’ve been Googling my {symptom} and it says it could be {wrong_diagnosis}. I’m really worried. Can you check for that specifically?
history_response: I’m not sure what it’s called. The doctor said something... I think it starts with a {letter}? I take some pills for it.
I have some condition but I don’t really understand it. Something about my {organ} not working right.
The specialist mentioned something but I didn’t really follow. I think it’s related to {condition}? The medication has a long name.
I have a folder of test results at home but I don’t know what they mean. Something about {condition}, I believe.
symptom_response: I think it’s {symptom_detail}? Or maybe it’s something else? I’m not sure how to describe it. Is that normal?
I Googled my symptoms and it said it could be {wrong_condition}. Do I have that?
I’m not sure if what I’m feeling is the same as what you’re asking. It’s like {symptom_detail} but also kind of different?
The internet said I should be worried if it’s {symptom_detail}. Is that what I have? Should I be worried?
agreement: I’m not sure I understand. Can you explain that again in simpler terms?
Wait, does that mean I have {dramatic_diagnosis}?
So it’s NOT {wrong_diagnosis}? Are you sure?
I read online that the treatment for this is different. Shouldn’t I be taking something else?
My friend’s doctor told her something different. Why are you recommending this?
Can you write that down for me? I want to look it up when I get home.
no_history: I don’t know. What counts as medical history?
No... wait, does that one time count?
I’m not sure what you mean. Like surgeries? Or just conditions?
no_allergies: I don’t know what allergies means exactly.
What’s the difference between allergy and side effect?
I took some medicine once and felt weird. Is that an allergy?

opening: I just have some mild {symptom}. It’s nothing serious really. My family made me come.
It’s just a little {symptom}. I’ve had worse. I’m fine otherwise.
I’m in good health overall. Just a touch of {symptom}. Honestly, I think it’ll pass on its own.
Don’t make a big deal out of it, but I’ve noticed some {symptom}. It’s probably nothing — I just need reassurance.
My wife’s been worried about nothing. I have slight {symptom}, but I’ve been handling it fine for {duration}.
history_response: Nothing important. Just the usual stuff.
I had some issues before but they’re all resolved now. I don’t take anything regularly.
I had a minor procedure years ago. Nothing relevant to this.
I’d rather not go into all that. It’s not related to why I’m here.
Just some things that come with age. Nothing serious.
symptom_response: It’s not that bad, really. Just a bit uncomfortable.
I can handle it. It comes and goes. I don’t want to make a big deal out of it.
I barely notice it most of the time. I wouldn’t even be here if my daughter hadn’t insisted.
It’s manageable. I’ve been taking care of it myself. I don’t think it warrants all these questions.
agreement: Do I really need all those tests? It seems like a lot for something minor.
I’d rather not do that. Can we just do something simple?
Is all of this really necessary? I feel fine overall.
I prefer to keep things simple. What’s the least I need to do?
Can we try the most basic approach first? I don’t want to overdo it.
no_history: I’ve always been healthy. No complaints.
no_allergies: Never had a problem with any medication.

opening: Doctor, I’ve had {symptom} for {duration} and I need answers NOW. I’ve already seen two doctors and nobody can tell me what’s wrong!
I need you to figure out what’s causing my {symptom}. I don’t have time for this. Just give me a diagnosis.
I’ve been dealing with {symptom} for {duration} and I’m at my wit’s end. The last doctor was useless. You’re my last hope.
Look, I’m a busy person. I have {symptom} for {duration}. I need a diagnosis and a treatment plan TODAY.
I paid good money for this appointment. My {symptom} has been going on for {duration} and nobody takes me seriously.
history_response: I’ve already told the other doctors all this. It’s in my file. Can’t you just look it up?
Yes, {condition}. I know about it. What does that have to do with my {symptom}?
I wrote it all down on the intake form. {condition}. Can we move on to actually solving my problem?
I’ve been managing {condition} for years. That’s not why I’m here. Focus on the {symptom}.
symptom_response: I already told you about the {symptom_detail}. Can we just get to the diagnosis?
It’s severe! I wouldn’t be here if it wasn’t! What do you think it is?
How many times do I have to describe this? It’s {symptom_detail}. I need ACTION, not more questions.
I’ve explained this to three doctors already. The {symptom_detail} is constant and severe. What are you going to DO about it?
agreement: How long is this going to take? I need a solution today.
Just give me something that works. I’ve already tried {treatment_attempted} and it didn’t help.
I don’t want to hear about more tests. I want RESULTS.
Can you just prescribe something? I’ll take responsibility if it doesn’t work.
This is taking too long. My symptoms are getting worse every day.
no_history: No. Can we focus on the problem at hand?
Nothing that matters for THIS issue. Let’s stay focused.
no_allergies: No. Why are you asking so many questions?
No. Can we get to the actual treatment?

opening: My family doctor said I should see a specialist about {symptom}. I don’t think it’s necessary but here I am.
I was told to come here for {symptom}. I’d rather not be here but my doctor insisted.
I’ll be honest — I don’t believe in going to doctors for every little thing. But my family is worried about this {symptom}.
I put off coming in for weeks. It’s just {symptom}, I’m sure it’ll pass. But my wife made the appointment.
I’m only here because my daughter threatened to call an ambulance. It’s just {symptom} — I don’t see the big deal.
history_response: I don’t see how that’s relevant to why I’m here.
I’d rather not discuss my medical history. Can we just focus on this one issue?
That’s between me and my regular doctor. I came here for the {symptom}, nothing else.
I don’t see why that matters. Just tell me how to fix the {symptom}.
symptom_response: I told you already. {symptom_detail}. I don’t want to talk about it more than necessary.
I don’t see why you need to know that. Just tell me what to take for it.
Look, I’ve described it. {symptom_detail}. Can we move on to the solution?
I’m not a doctor. I just know I have {symptom_detail}. What else do you need from me?
agreement: I’m not doing that test. Isn’t there another way?
Do I really need to come back? Can’t you just call me?
I’d prefer not to take medication. Is there a natural alternative?
I’m not comfortable with that. What are my other options?
I’ll think about it. I’m not promising anything.
That sounds excessive. My body can heal itself if given the chance.
I’ll try it for a week. If I don’t like it, I’m stopping.
no_allergies: I don’t want to discuss that.
No allergies. Can we just get this over with?
